title: Wrong result produced when left joining information_schema tables
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_db.users (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(100), created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
CREATE TABLE test_db.orders (order_id INT PRIMARY KEY, user_id INT, amount DECIMAL(10, 2), FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES test_db.users(id));
SELECT u.name, o.amount FROM test_db.users u LEFT JOIN test_db.orders o ON u.id = o.user_id WHERE o.amount IS NULL;
INSERT INTO test_db.orders (order_id, user_id, amount) VALUES (1, 1, 100.00), (2, 2, NULL);
key_bug_features: Left joining information_schema tables produces wrong results
